title: Implement workspace_initialize(cwd) and register it in the tool layer
summary: Add a dedicated `workspace_initialize(cwd)` tool that binds the canonical project root once per server session, supports Zed multi-project startup when process CWD is '/', and preserves the safety invariant that file-backed operations never resolve artifacts under '/.agent'.
acceptance_criteria: A new `workspace_initialize(cwd)` tool is exposed through the runtime tool layer.
`workspace_initialize(cwd)` validates the provided cwd, rejects '/' as invalid, and binds the repo root when no root is currently initialized.
Calling `workspace_initialize(cwd)` again with the same root succeeds as a no-op, while rebinding to a different root fails with a clear error.
The initialization flow integrates cleanly with startup/runtime behavior for both normal single-project launches and Zed multi-project launches that start with process CWD='/' .

steps.description: Define the tool contract for `workspace_initialize(cwd)`, including success shape, same-root reinitialization behavior, and conflicting-root rejection.
Implement runtime binding logic that routes `workspace_initialize(cwd)` to the repo-context initialization path.
Register the new tool in the exposed tool list and ensure it is callable through the JSON-RPC tool execution flow.
Verify that normal non-root startup remains backward compatible while unresolved '/' startup can be recovered by the new initialization tool.

notes: This ticket focuses on the initialization tool itself, not on updating every root-dependent operation.
The implementation must keep the one-server-one-project invariant explicit and enforceable.
Prefer keeping initialization semantics centralized rather than duplicating bind logic across unrelated components.
